mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals expanding output capacity and aggregate supply, which boosts corporate earnings and investor confidence, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC of INDPRO monthly changes on forward 20-day returns is below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 5% after a period of low unemployment signals the onset of a recessionary labor market contraction, reducing consumer spending and earnings expectations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:historical recessions typically preceded by rising unemployment
failure_condition: After UNRATE > 5%, average forward 60-day return is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.10) or hit rate of negative returns is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is rising while industrial production is falling, the joint signal of tightening monetary policy and contracting economic output creates a recessionary headwind that depresses equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint condition (FEDFUNDS > its 6-month median and INDPRO < its 6-month median) does not produce a negative median forward 20-day return over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: In a low federal funds rate regime (FEDFUNDS < 2%), rising housing starts signal robust residential investment and consumer confidence, which feeds into positive equity returns over a 20-day horizon; outside this regime the signal is muted.
failure_condition: In periods where FEDFUNDS < 2% and HOUST 3-month change is positive, the forward 20-day return is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) compared to periods with negative HOUST change.

mechanism_narrative: A rising real GDP (GDPC1) indicates broad economic expansion that lifts corporate profits and stock prices over a quarterly horizon; this is a fundamental growth signal that is always active.
failure_condition: The quarterly change in GDPC1 does not have a positive correlation with forward 60-day SPX returns over the full sample (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) exceeds 3% year-over-year, it signals overheating and anticipated monetary tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: In periods where CPIAUCSL YoY > 3%, the median forward 20-day return is not significantly negative (one-sided Wilcoxon test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in nonfarm payrolls and retail sales indicates a robust labor market and consumer spending, which together drive corporate earnings and stock market appreciation over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint condition (both PAYEMS and RSAFS above their 12-month median) does not yield a median forward 60-day return above the unconditional median (bootstrap test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A falling unemployment rate reflects tightening labor market conditions that boost consumer sentiment and corporate sales, leading to positive short-term equity returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: The one-month change in UNRATE does not have a significant negative correlation with forward 5-day returns (p > 0.10) over any 3-year rolling window.
